docs.title: oeis-code
docs.text: Mini OEIS in Python with PARI/GP support.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Welcome to the oeis-code documentation. This library allows you to interact with the Online Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences (OEIS) programmatically using native Python and PARI/GP backends.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install oeis-code

from oeis_code import get

# Get the 10th Fibonacci number
get("A000045", 10)  # Returns 55

Check out the Usage Guide and the API Reference to see all the available classes and functions.

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: This guide covers the core functionalities of oeis-code.

docs.title: Getting Sequence Terms
docs.text:
The primary interface is the get() function. It evaluates the sequence at a given index.

from oeis_code import get

# Get the 10th Fibonacci number
val = get("A000045", 10)
print(val) # 55

docs.title: Adding Sequences
docs.text:
To add sequences to oeis-code, you register them in the src/oeis_code/sequences/ directory:

from oeis_code.registry import register

@register("A000045")
def sequence(n):
    if n <= 1:
        return n
    return sequence(n - 1) + sequence(n - 2)

docs.title: Backends
docs.text: oeis-code provides fallbacks and PARI/GP bindings for high performance sequence calculations. Ensure you have pari-gp installed on your system to take advantage of the advanced cypari2 backend.

docs.title: get(seq_id, n, backend='auto')
docs.text:
Get the n-th term of an OEIS sequence.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default seq_id str

The OEIS identifier (e.g., 'A000045').

required n int

The index of the term to retrieve.

required backend BackendType

The backend to use ('auto', 'pari', or 'python'). 'auto' will prioritize PARI/GP if available.

'auto'

Returns:

Type Description Any

The n-th term of the sequence.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If the sequence ID is not found in the registry.

Source code in src/oeis_code/core.py 
def get(seq_id: str, n: int, backend: BackendType = "auto") -> Any:
    """
    Get the n-th term of an OEIS sequence.

    Args:
        seq_id: The OEIS identifier (e.g., 'A000045').
        n: The index of the term to retrieve.
        backend: The backend to use ('auto', 'pari', or 'python').
            'auto' will prioritize PARI/GP if available.

    Returns:
        The n-th term of the sequence.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If the sequence ID is not found in the registry.
    """
    if seq_id not in registry:
        raise ValueError(f"Sequence {seq_id} not implemented")

    func = registry[seq_id]

    if backend == "auto":
        try:
            if pari_backend.AVAILABLE:
                return func(n, backend="pari")
        except Exception:
            pass
        return func(n, backend="python")

    return func(n, backend=backend)

docs.title: register(seq_id)
docs.text:
Decorator to register a sequence function in the registry.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default seq_id str

The OEIS sequence identifier (e.g., 'A000045').

required

Returns:

Type Description Callable[[Callable[..., Any]], Callable[..., Any]]

The decorator function.

Source code in src/oeis_code/registry.py 
def register(seq_id: str) -> Callable[[Callable[..., Any]], Callable[..., Any]]:
    """
    Decorator to register a sequence function in the registry.

    Args:
        seq_id: The OEIS sequence identifier (e.g., 'A000045').

    Returns:
        The decorator function.
    """

    def decorator(func: Callable[..., Any]) -> Callable[..., Any]:
        registry[seq_id] = func
        return func

    return decorator

docs.title: fibonacci_term(n)
docs.text:
Calculate the n-th Fibonacci number (0-indexed). A000045: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, ...

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default n int

The index of the term.

required

Returns:

Type Description int

The n-th Fibonacci number.

Source code in src/oeis_code/utils/math.py 
def fibonacci_term(n: int) -> int:
    """
    Calculate the n-th Fibonacci number (0-indexed).
    A000045: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, ...

    Args:
        n: The index of the term.

    Returns:
        The n-th Fibonacci number.
    """
    if n == 0:
        return 0
    if n == 1:
        return 1
    a, b = 0, 1
    for _ in range(2, n + 1):
        a, b = b, a + b
    return b

docs.title: is_prime(n)
docs.text:
Check if a number is prime using trial division.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default n int

The number to check.

required

Returns:

Type Description bool

True if n is prime, False otherwise.

Source code in src/oeis_code/utils/math.py 
def is_prime(n: int) -> bool:
    """
    Check if a number is prime using trial division.

    Args:
        n: The number to check.

    Returns:
        True if n is prime, False otherwise.
    """
    if n < 2:
        return False
    if n == 2 or n == 3:
        return True
    if n % 2 == 0 or n % 3 == 0:
        return False

    # Trial division up to sqrt(n)
    for i in range(5, int(math.sqrt(n)) + 1, 6):
        if n % i == 0 or n % (i + 2) == 0:
            return False
    return True
